content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me examine atom C:13 (carbon with index 13) carefully.

Looking at the image, I can see atom C:13 (orange/gold sphere labeled "C:13") and its direct connections:
- It is bonded to N:11 (nitrogen, index 11) - blue sphere
- It is bonded to C:10 (carbon, index 10) - orange/gold sphere  
- It is bonded to H:14 (hydrogen, index 14) - white sphere
- It is bonded to H:18 (hydrogen, index 18) - white sphere

I can clearly see these four bonds from atom C:13 in the 3D structure. The connections are visible as the lines/bonds between the spheres.

From the file name "geomconf_4766.xyz", I can determine that the geomconf_index is 4766.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom C:13 are:
- Atom index 11: N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 10: C (carbon)
- Atom index 14: H (hydrogen)
- Atom index 18: H (hydrogen)

And the geomconf_index for this molecule is 4766.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 13, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
